mappings.rationale: Constitutional law governing the organization and functions of the state is the central subject (A.1).
Examines constitutional law in a parliamentary system, including the Bill of Rights and common law principles (A.1).
Laws governing public administration -- including judicial review, tribunals, and ombudsmen -- are the central subject (A.2).

mappings.vsiAuthor: Stella Z. Theodoulou, Ravi K. Roy
mappings.rationale: The organization, powers, and functions of public administrative authorities are examined (A.2).
Laws governing relations among sovereign states, treaties, and international institutions are the focus (B).
The UN represents the most significant attempt to create a supranational legislative and executive authority (B.2).
EU law represents a distinctive attempt to create supranational legislative and judicial authority (B.2, B.3).
Laws attempting to limit war crimes and crimes against humanity, and laws promoting public welfare, are examined (B.5, D).
Laws governing offenses by military forces and persons under military discipline are the central subject (C.2).
Principles of criminal law and the criminal justice process are examined (C.1, F.2.a).
The study of criminal law, crime prevention, and white-collar crime is addressed (C).
Courts, the legal system, and judicial procedure are broadly addressed (F).
Comparisons between common law and civil law systems illuminate criminal law doctrines (C.1).
The organization and administration of the legal system -- including courts, judges, and judicial procedure -- are examined (F.1, F.2).
The organization and role of the highest court in a federal judicial system is the central subject (F.1).
The jury system as part of criminal and civil judicial procedure is examined (F.2).
Habeas corpus as a fundamental legal procedure protecting liberty and constraining state power is the central subject (F.2.a).
Laws providing for general social security and welfare are examined (D.1).
Laws promoting public health and safety are addressed (D.2).
Laws regulating the health, safety, and welfare of workers are the focus (D.3).
Laws governing health, consent, negligence, and patient welfare are examined (D.2).
Public welfare laws related to environmental protection and public safety are addressed (D.2).
Laws governing taxation -- including income tax, corporation tax, and tax evasion -- are directly relevant (E).
The rule of law as a foundational principle underlying constitutional and administrative law is the central subject (A).
Data protection and privacy rights as matters of public law and constitutional protection are explored (A.1).
Freedom of speech as a constitutional right and its legal limits are examined (A.1).
The attempt to limit and punish war crimes and crimes against humanity is a central theme (B.5).
Scientific methods used in criminal investigations and the administration of criminal justice are examined (C, F.2.a).
Psychological methods applied in criminal proceedings and judicial processes are explored (F.2).
Blasphemy laws as a form of criminal law governing religious expression are examined (C.1).
